fact: The first official cricket Test match was played in 1877 between Australia and England at the Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG). Australia won by 45 runs.
The ICC Cricket World Cup was first held in 1975 in England, and the West Indies won the inaugural title, defeating Australia in the final at Lord's.
Sachin Tendulkar holds the record for the most international centuries in cricket history, with 100 centuries across Test and One Day International formats.
A standard Twenty20 (T20) cricket match consists of a maximum of 20 overs per side, making it the shortest widely played format of the sport.
The FIFA World Cup was first held in 1930. Uruguay hosted and won the tournament, defeating Argentina 4-2 in the final match in Montevideo.
Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup a record five times, in 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, and 2002.
A standard football (soccer) match consists of two 45-minute halves, for a total of 90 minutes of regulation play plus stoppage time.
Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo are widely regarded among the greatest goal scorers in football history, both surpassing 800 career goals for club and country.
The Thomas Cup, established in 1948, is the premier international men's team badminton championship, held every two years.
India won its first-ever Thomas Cup title in 2022, defeating 14-time champions Indonesia 3-0 in the final held in Bangkok.
Badminton has been an official Olympic sport since the 1992 Barcelona Games, featuring singles and doubles events for both men and women.
A badminton match is typically played as the best of three games, with each game played to 21 points (win by at least 2 points).
The four Grand Slam tournaments in tennis are the Australian Open, the French Open (Roland Garros), Wimbledon, and the US Open.
Wimbledon is the oldest tennis tournament in the world, first held in 1877, and is the only Grand Slam still played on grass courts.
Novak Djokovic, Rafael Nadal, and Roger Federer are widely regarded among the greatest men's singles players in history, each having won more than 20 Grand Slam singles titles.
At the Grand Slam tournaments, men's singles matches are played as best of five sets, while women's singles matches are played as best of three sets.
Basketball was invented in December 1891 by Dr. James Naismith, a physical education instructor in Springfield, Massachusetts.
The National Basketball Association (NBA) was founded in 1946 as the Basketball Association of America (BAA) before adopting its current name in 1949.
A standard NBA game consists of four quarters of 12 minutes each, for 48 minutes of regulation play.
Kareem Abdul-Jabbar held the NBA's all-time career scoring record for nearly four decades before LeBron James surpassed it in February 2023.
